title: notes/qa/** is gitignored — QA artifact files cannot be committed
summary: S092 design §7 designates notes/qa/** as QA's writable output directory for screenshots, console dumps and repro artifacts. However, .gitignore contains the pattern Notes/ and git config core.ignoreCase=true, which causes git to silently ignore all notes/ paths as well (case-insensitive match). Any file QA writes to notes/qa/ will never be tracked, making the artifact capture part of the workflow (screenshots, log excerpts) permanently broken. Companion to QA-2026-05-20-002.
repro: Open .gitignore at repo root — locate the pattern `Notes/` on line 30.
Observe: git reports `.gitignore:30:Notes/  notes/qa/test.txt` — the file is ignored despite the lowercase path.
Run: git config core.ignoreCase — returns `true`, confirming case-insensitive .gitignore matching.
Attempt to create notes/qa/QA-2026-05-20-001/screenshot.png and git add it — git silently skips the file.
expected: notes/qa/** is a tracked path so QA agents can commit screenshots, console dumps, and other repro artifacts alongside ticket JSON files.
actual: .gitignore pattern Notes/ with core.ignoreCase=true matches notes/ too. Every file written to notes/qa/ is silently ignored by git. The field screenshot in qa-ticket.schema.json can reference a path but the file will never land in the repository.
